title: Campaign Shake Up
weakest_section: 11:00-12:00 range with water fountain physical comedy

jokes.text: When I say your names, I want you to respond. Dylan. Well, Newport's poll numbers have been plummeting.
jokes.setup: Host asks Dylan to respond when name is called
jokes.punchline: Dylan immediately launches into political analysis without acknowledging the instruction
jokes.explanation: Clean subversion of expectation with good economy, but not particularly character-specific

jokes.text: [Laughs] That's a hilarious image. A foot in a mouth. It's a common expression. Okay.
jokes.setup: Someone uses common expression 'puts his foot in his mouth'
jokes.punchline: Dylan finds the literal image hilariously absurd, then awkwardly retreats when corrected
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing overly literal thinking, solid structure

jokes.text: He's like a brilliant, sexy, little hummingbird.
jokes.setup: Leslie praising Ben's campaign management skills
jokes.punchline: Escalates to bizarre metaphor comparing him to a 'sexy hummingbird'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope line - unexpected metaphor, character-specific romantic obliviousness, highly quotable

jokes.text: What did you just say? Nothing. Keep up the good work. You're doing great. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Ben catches Leslie's bizarre hummingbird comment
jokes.punchline: Leslie's obvious deflection and nervous chuckling
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up beat showing Leslie's awareness of her weirdness

jokes.text: The 'grond' father, he's like-- It doesn't matter. He's important, and he's old.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to make a clever 'Godfather' pun about elderly man
jokes.punchline: Pun doesn't work, she gives up immediately and states facts bluntly
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's earnestness overriding her attempt at cleverness

jokes.text: Please don't get up. Don't worry. I can't. Just joking. I can get up. But it's difficult. But I can do it, but it is hard.
jokes.setup: Leslie politely tells elderly man not to get up
jokes.punchline: Ned's overly detailed explanation of his mobility limitations escalates awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, though not particularly original

jokes.text: Look, I don't have a lot of time. Oh, God. I'm so sorry. Is it... cancer? No, I don't have a lot of time before my swim aerobics.
jokes.setup: Elderly man says he doesn't have a lot of time
jokes.punchline: Leslie assumes he's dying, but he means swim class
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect setup/payoff structure and Leslie's characteristic overthinking

jokes.text: Not enough ramps is the number three complaint among Pawnee seniors... Right behind 'everything hurts' and 'I'm dying.'
jokes.setup: Leslie listing senior citizen complaints in professional tone
jokes.punchline: Escalates from practical to existentially dark complaints
jokes.explanation: Sharp escalation with dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, very quotable

jokes.text: Great exercise, and it literally lets me see the world from a different perspective.
jokes.setup: Chris doing upside-down sit-ups
jokes.punchline: Takes literal physical perspective as profound metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger line showing his earnest New Age philosophy applied to everything

jokes.text: The world's my gymnasium, Ron. But I will stop if it makes you uncomfortable. Could you hold me while I dismount?
jokes.setup: Chris being characteristically over-the-top about exercise
jokes.punchline: Asks stoic Ron to help him down from upside-down position
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Chris and Ron, awkward physical comedy

jokes.text: I work hard to make sure my department is as small and ineffective as possible.
jokes.setup: Chris asking why Parks department is understaffed
jokes.punchline: Ron proudly admits to sabotaging government efficiency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line - anti-government sentiment delivered with pride

jokes.text: Reminds me of when my dad made me choose which of my pet calves to slaughter with my own hands for my sixth birthday. I couldn't choose, so I slaughtered both of them. And they were delicious.
jokes.setup: Ron comparing current dilemma to childhood choice
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurdly dark childhood story told matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron Swanson absurdist backstory - perfect escalation, character-specific dark humor, highly quotable

jokes.text: Like I always say, stairs are a young man's game.
jokes.setup: Leslie introducing her ramp initiative
jokes.punchline: Delivers fake folksy wisdom as if it's common knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's tendency to create fake sayings

jokes.text: You have the same name, Leslie. He lost a third of his body in a motorcycle accident. The middle third. But they sutured the hell out of him. He's fine now. Much shorter, but a good-looking, young, flat man.
jokes.setup: Ned saying Leslie reminds him of his brother
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre details about brother losing his torso but being 'fine'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation with perfect deadpan delivery - 'flat man' is inspired

jokes.text: I told her sorry. I don't talk politics after dinner.
jokes.setup: High-powered DC operative wants meeting at 5pm
jokes.punchline: Ben treats it like social etiquette issue
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's small-town adjustment

jokes.text: She's eating egg salad with Colin Powell. That's the most impressive thing I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Looking at photo of Jennifer Barkley with Colin Powell
jokes.punchline: Leslie focuses on the mundane detail of egg salad rather than the powerful connection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - missing the point in endearing way, focusing on food detail

jokes.text: As a candidate, I appreciate your strategic mind, but as woman, all I care about is your slight but powerful body.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving Ben professional compliment
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines it with inappropriate physical comment
jokes.explanation: Great callback to earlier 'hummingbird' comment, shows Leslie's romantic distraction

jokes.text: This is the first time you've said my entire name correctly.
jokes.setup: Chris says 'Hello, Ann Perkins'
jokes.punchline: Ann points out this is rare occurrence
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established running gag about Chris's name issues

jokes.text: Kiss one water-fountain drinker, you're kissing everyone in Pawnee. Including him.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining Pawnee's disgusting water fountain habits
jokes.punchline: Connects it to kissing chain, presumably pointing at gross person
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with logical progression and visual component

jokes.text: Hello, Marta. We would like our regular table, but we want you to set it up a little fancier than normal because we're trying to impress someone. You mean Ms. Barkley. She's already here. Last booth.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for fancy table to impress visitor
jokes.punchline: Waitress already knows exactly who they're meeting
jokes.explanation: Solid small-town joke showing everyone knows everything

jokes.text: Bobby Newport's daddy called me out of the blue. I'd never heard of Bobby Newport before, but it became clear in two seconds this guy is a moron. So I was, like, 'sorry, Nick. Your son is a moron. There's no way.' Then he offered me a crazy amount of money.
jokes.setup: Jennifer explaining how she got hired
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about calling client's son a moron to his face
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Jennifer's ruthless honesty

jokes.text: Do you know Joe Biden? Oh, mm-hmm. He's on my celebrity sex list. Well, he is my celebrity sex list. Oh, trust me. You can do better than Joe. [Laughing] Oh, no. I don't-- I don't think I can.
jokes.setup: Jennifer mentioning Joe Biden
jokes.punchline: Leslie's entire celebrity sex list is just Joe Biden, delivered with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - political obsession extends to romance, delivered with earnest conviction

jokes.text: I'm Leslie Knope and when I grow up, I want to be a unicorn princess or a city councilor. Wrong. Not in my town, kid. City council's a real job for a real adult.
jokes.setup: Attack ad shows Leslie in childish light
jokes.punchline: Jennifer appears to lecture the 'child' Leslie
jokes.explanation: Effective attack ad parody with visual component

jokes.text: And I should listen to you always because you are a man genius with a taut, narrow frame like a sexy, elf king.
jokes.setup: Leslie agreeing with Ben's strategic advice
jokes.punchline: Another bizarre metaphor comparing Ben to 'sexy elf king'
jokes.explanation: Another classic Leslie metaphor, building the running gag of her romantic distraction

jokes.text: Do you wish I were taller or-- what's going on?
jokes.setup: Ben noticing Leslie's strange compliments
jokes.punchline: His confused, slightly insecure response
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Ben's growing awareness of Leslie's behavior

jokes.text: Andy is our official water-fountain tester.
jokes.setup: Ann introducing fountain testing concept
jokes.punchline: Andy demonstrates by immediately putting mouth on fountain spout
jokes.explanation: Good use of Andy's character as willing test subject for gross behavior

jokes.text: Full disclosure. Ann and I are romantically intertwined. Oh, God. So let's not be surprised if she picks my idea.
jokes.setup: Tom announcing conflict of interest
jokes.punchline: Ann's horrified reaction to his overconfident announcement
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his ego and romantic delusion

jokes.text: This... is the Voss Water Butler by Tumi. And only $600 a bag.
jokes.setup: Tom presenting water fountain alternative
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously expensive luxury water as practical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford solution - unnecessarily expensive and impractical

jokes.text: Do I look like I drink water?
jokes.setup: Asked for water fountain input
jokes.punchline: Donna's dismissive response implying she only drinks fancy beverages
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna line - economical, character-specific, dismissive attitude

jokes.text: Yoo-hoo. I'm over here. Just about to start my presentation. I-- uh-oh, stairs. What do I do? How do I get from here to there? I'm not a professional mountain climber.
jokes.setup: Leslie demonstrating accessibility problem
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic presentation of simple stair navigation
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy but somewhat predictable presentation format

jokes.text: Ramps are an idiot's game. This is nothing more than a worse version of Bobby Newport's plan.
jokes.setup: Leslie finishes ramp presentation
jokes.punchline: Jennifer dismisses it as inferior to Newport's plan
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure but not particularly original

jokes.text: Why don't we just turn up the pressure? That way you don't have to put your mouth so close. All right, here we go. Ah, stop! Honey, I'm sorry. That'll teach you to pay attention.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggests increasing water pressure
jokes.punchline: Predictably backfires, soaking someone (likely Andy)
jokes.explanation: Predictable physical comedy, Jerry's typical bad idea backfiring

jokes.text: Oh, my-- dude, what the hell?
jokes.setup: Andy gets soaked by fountain
jokes.punchline: His surprised reaction to getting drenched
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction to physical gag, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Help, everything I'm wearing is suede! Everything I'm wearing is suede, everything I'm wearing is suede!
jokes.setup: Water balloon fight chaos in office
jokes.punchline: Tom's panic about his expensive clothes getting wet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - materialistic panic about fashion, repetitive escalation works

jokes.text: Kamikaze! Oh. [Laughs]
jokes.setup: Chaos continuing in office
jokes.punchline: Andy announces dramatic entrance then presumably fails at it
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy moment - announces big action then laughs at own failure

jokes.text: He's making out with some floozy in Majorca. Sorry, I wish I was better at this. Then you could be making out with some floozy in Majorca.
jokes.setup: Leslie complaining about Bobby Newport's vacation
jokes.punchline: Ben's self-deprecating response connecting his campaign skills to romantic rewards
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Newport criticism with Ben's modest self-awareness

jokes.text: We may be in for a good, old-fashioned shake-up.
jokes.setup: Discussing potential new hire
jokes.punchline: Ron using episode title phrase, delivered dryly
jokes.explanation: Subtle meta reference to episode title, works with Ron's understated delivery

jokes.text: You two-faced, carpetbagging-- Hi, Leslie. Good to see you. Hi, nice to see you, too. You are a two-faced, carpet-- You want anything? Anything? It's on me.
jokes.setup: Leslie storms in ready to confront Jennifer
jokes.punchline: Jennifer's charm repeatedly derails Leslie's prepared insults
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing Leslie thrown off by Jennifer's friendliness

jokes.text: Let me get my insults out. I've been practicing it the whole way over here.
jokes.setup: Leslie's confrontation being derailed
jokes.punchline: Her honest admission about rehearsing the confrontation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - earnest honesty even in confrontation, endearingly awkward

jokes.text: Water balloon fight. Fair enough.
jokes.setup: Chris asking about 'hands-on brainstorming session'
jokes.punchline: Ann's blunt correction and Chris's acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good callback with efficient deflation of corporate speak

jokes.text: I think Ben's already filling the Leslie void. Oh! Oh! I'll give it up for that.
jokes.setup: Discussion of filling Leslie's role
jokes.punchline: Tom's sexual innuendo about Ben and Leslie
jokes.explanation: Obvious sexual pun, relies on cheap double entendre rather than clever wordplay

jokes.text: It's 'Perd,' first of all, and no. Maybe. No. That's not a terrible idea.
jokes.setup: Jennifer guessing Leslie's strategy
jokes.punchline: Leslie's contradictory responses showing she's flustered
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie outmaneuvered and flustered

jokes.text: JJ's doesn't have a cappuccino maker. Oh, it does now. I bought them one. Course you did.
jokes.setup: Fancy coffee appearing at local diner
jokes.punchline: Jennifer casually bought restaurant equipment, Leslie's resigned response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jennifer's casual display of power and resources

jokes.text: Or maybe she thinks that we think that she thinks that, so we won't do it. But she knows that I know that she knows that-- She's in our heads.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to analyze Jennifer's strategy
jokes.punchline: Gets lost in recursive thinking, Leslie cuts through with simple truth
jokes.explanation: Good escalation into absurd over-thinking, Leslie's simple conclusion works well

jokes.text: Because she's April, and she doesn't want you to think that she actually cares about something.
jokes.setup: Chris confused why April gave Andy credit
jokes.punchline: Andy's surprisingly insightful explanation of April's psychology
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Andy showing unexpected emotional intelligence about April

jokes.text: Oh, you're still here? Yes.
jokes.setup: Chris asking April if she should go
jokes.punchline: April's blunt confirmation that yes, she should leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - economical, rude, character-appropriate

jokes.text: I don't know where she went the first time.
jokes.setup: Jennifer says 'There she goes again'
jokes.punchline: Perd's literal confusion about metaphorical expression
jokes.explanation: Great Perd moment - his literal interpretation of common expressions is perfect character comedy

jokes.text: And that woman that he is with is an antiland-mine advocate. I didn't realize that you were proland-mine.
jokes.setup: Jennifer defending Newport's companion as advocate
jokes.punchline: Perd's logical but absurd assumption about Leslie's position
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Perd's logic creates absurd political position, very quotable

jokes.text: I am not here to declare a winner, but Jennifer is the winner.
jokes.setup: Perd claiming neutrality
jokes.punchline: Immediately declares winner anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Perd contradiction - claims neutrality then immediately abandons it

jokes.text: I am going to get drunk, and then I'm going to order a three-course meal where each course is made of dessert.
jokes.setup: Leslie dealing with defeat on TV
jokes.punchline: Her comfort plan escalates from drinking to absurd dessert meal
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie response to crisis - specific, food-focused, escalates to absurd comfort plan

jokes.text: Oh, that thing I said on the show? Oh, no. That was mostly made-up. Don't challenge me on that. That will backfire.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about the European company story
jokes.punchline: Jennifer casually admits to lying on TV, warns against calling her out
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jennifer's ruthless confidence and casual dishonesty

jokes.text: I don't want to do things. I want to not do things, just like you taught me.
jokes.setup: Ron asking April to take on more responsibility
jokes.punchline: April citing Ron's own philosophy against him
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - April using Ron's own teachings against him, perfect character consistency

jokes.text: Okay... But only because you begged.
jokes.setup: Ron asking nicely for April's help
jokes.punchline: April reframes it as begging to maintain her superiority
jokes.explanation: Good April moment - agreeing to help but maintaining her attitude and face
